title: salix glauca var. macounii
intro: Salix glauca var. macounii, also known as Macoun's willow or Macoun's gray willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The medicinal properties of Salix glauca var. macounii include its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain due to its salicylic acid content, making it a natural alternative to aspirin. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate headaches, fever, and rheumatic pain, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. Salix glauca var. macounii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, with the bark being the most commonly used part for medicinal purposes.
